Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Amended Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: DEFENDANT ARAMARK FACILITY SERVICES, LLC's (INCORRECTLY SUED AS ARAMARK MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.) Motion To Compel Responses To Amended Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ARAMARK SERVICES, INC'S (HEREAFTER EITHER "DEFENDANT" OR "ARAMARK") MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS ("RFP") NOS. 9, 15, 18, 19, 20, 26, 36, 37, 38 & 41 FROM PLAINTIFF RAYMOND OIYEMHONLAN (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "OIYEMHONLAN"} IS GRANTED AS FOLLOWS:
(1) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSE TO RFP NOS. 9, 18 ,19 & 20 IDENTIFYING THE SPECIFIC DOCUMENTS IT HAS PREVIOUSLY PRODUCED OR INTENDS TO PRODUCE WHICH ARE SPECIFICALLY RESPONSIVE TO RFP NOS. 9,18,19 & 20 AND THAT SUCH PRODUCTION CONSTITUTES THE PRODUCTION OF ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR THE RFP..
(2) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH A FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSE TO RFP NO. 15 AND PRODUCE ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS FOR THE PERIOD FROM JANUARY 1, 2016 TO THE PRESENT. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT ON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE HE WILL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ENABLE DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM AND THE BASIS FOR HIS CLAIM THAT HIS OBJECTION WAS NOT WAIVED BY HIS UNTIMELY RESPONSE TO THE RFP.
(3) WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER PLAINTIFF WILL PROVIDE DEFENDANT WITH A FURTHER VERIFIED RESPONSE TO RFP NO. 26 & 41. TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF IS WITHHOLDING ANY RESPONSIVE DOCUMENT ON A CLAIM OF PRIVILEGE HE WILL IDENTIFY THE DOCUMENTS WITH SUFFICIENT SPECIFICITY TO ENABLE DEFENDANT TO CHALLENGE THE CLAIM AND THE BASIS FOR HIS CLAIM THAT HIS OBJECTION WAS NOT WAIVED BY HIS UNTIMELY RESPONSE TO THE RFP.
(4) PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO RFP NOS 36, 37 & 38 ARE OVERRULED. WITHIN FIFTEEN (15) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER, PLAINTIFF WILL DELIVER TO DEFENDANT VERIFIED CODE COMPLIANT RESPONSES TO THOSE RFP NOS 36, 37 & 38 INCLUDING DELIVERY OF ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS CALLED FOR BY EACH RFP.
(5) PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AS AGAINST ANY PARTY TO THIS LITIGATION.

COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER CONSISTENT WITH THESE TENTATIVE RULINGS, SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85@gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING REGARDLESS WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS SIGNIFIED HIS AGREEMENT AS TO FORM.

Judge Pro Tem: Paul Renne. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF RAYMOND OIYEMHONLAN Motion To Quash Subpoena For Production Of Business Records

PLAINTIFF RAYMOND OLYEMHONLAN (HEREAFTER EITHER "PLAINTIFF" OR "OLYEMHONLAN") MOTION TO QUASH THE BUSINESS RECORDS ONLY SUBPOENA ISSUED TO THIRD PARTY UCSF IS DENIED AS FOLLOWS.

(1) WITHIN THIRTY (30) CALENDAYS DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER UCSF SHALL DELIVER TO THE PARTIES THE RECORDS REQUESTED IN THE SUBPOENA PREVIOUSLY SERVED UPON IT BY DEFENDANT ARAMARK MANAGEMENT SERVICES, INC.

(2) IF PLAINTIFF WISHES TO HAVE THE PRODUCTION SUBJECT T0 A PROTECTIVE ORDER GOVERNING THE PRODUCTION AND USE OF THE SUBPOENAED RECORDS THE PARTIES SHOULD MEET WITHIN FIVE (5) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DATE OF THIS ORDER FOR THE PURPOSE OF ARRIVING A STIPULATED PROTECTIVE ORDER FOR SUBMISSION TO THE COURT FOR SIGNING AND FILING. IF THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO ARRIVE AT A STIPLATED ORDER WITHIN THE TIME FRAME SPECIFIED ABOVE, EACH WILL SUBMIT TO THE COURT THEIR RESPECTIVE ORDER WITHIN TEN (10) CALENDAR DAYS FROM THE DAE OF THIS ORDERAND THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER WHICH WILL GOVERN THE DISCOVERY IN THIS MATTER.

(3) THE COURT WILL IMPOSE NO MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST ANY PARTY TO THIS LITIGATION.56
C0UNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHOULD PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER SUBMIT A COPY TO COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM AND DELIVER A COPY IN WORDFORM TO THE COURT (rennepar85.gmail.com) IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING ON 2/6/24 REGARDLESS OF WHETHER IT HAS RECEIVED AN APPROVAL FROM PLAINTIFF.
